task_completion.description: CoppeliaSim is opened and a robot arm scene is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CoppeliaSim GUI is visible on the screen with a robot arm present in the 3D scene.

task_completion.description: A dynamically enabled payload is programmatically created and attached to the robot arm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script (Python or Lua) is visible showing the programmatic creation of a shape/payload, or the CoppeliaSim scene hierarchy shows a new dynamically enabled object attached to the end-effector.

task_completion.description: The robot arm is moved through multiple distinct static poses with varying horizontal reach.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Console output, script execution logs, or sequential screenshots showing the robot arm moving to different reach positions and measuring joint torques.

task_completion.description: The torque_data.csv file is created with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/torque_data.csv with the header row: config_id, ee_x, ee_y, ee_z, reach_m, baseline_torque, loaded_torque, delta_torque.

task_completion.description: The CSV file contains data for at least 10 distinct static poses.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The torque_data.csv file shows at least 10 rows of numerical data (excluding the header) corresponding to the different configurations.

task_completion.description: The payload_estimation.json file is created with the required fields.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the contents of /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/payload_estimation.json containing the keys: total_configs, analyzed_joint_name, estimated_mass_kg, and method_description.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran a simulation to gather data rather than fabricating the CSV/JSON files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of a script interacting with the CoppeliaSim API (e.g., sim.setJointTargetPosition, sim.getJointForce) or the CoppeliaSim simulation playing/logging data in the console.

integrity.description: The payload was created via code as requested, not manually added via the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of code (Python/Lua) that spawns a primitive shape and sets its dynamic properties, rather than the agent using the 'Add -> Primitive shape' GUI menus.

integrity.description: The exported data contains realistic, calculated floating-point values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV and JSON files contain precise floating-point numbers for torques, reach, and estimated mass, rather than simple integers or placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Minimum required configurations (poses): 10. Expected CSV output path: /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/torque_data.csv. Expected JSON output path: /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/payload_estimation.json.
